task: I’m putting together a short take-home note for clients after a session, and I want it to be something they can actually use later. What’s a simple way to organize it so the next steps are easy to remember and follow?

turns.agent_response:
A simple way is to keep it to **3–4 short sections** that answer: **what happened, what to do, what to watch for, and when to follow up**.

### Easy take-home note format
**1) Today’s focus**
- 1 sentence on what you worked on
- Example: “We reviewed your shoulder exercises and how to pace them through the day.”

**2) Next steps**
- 2–3 clear actions
- Use plain verbs: **do, avoid, repeat, rest**
- Example:
  - Do the exercises once daily
  - Keep movements gentle for now
  - Take breaks if pain rises

**3) What to watch for**
- 1–3 signs that mean they should slow down or contact you
- Example: “If pain increases sharply, swelling worsens, or symptoms last into tomorrow…”

**4) Follow-up plan**
- When to check in
- Example: “We’ll review progress next visit” or “Message if things feel worse after 3 days.”

### A useful rule
Write it so a client can **read it in under 30 seconds** and still know:
- **what to do today**
- **what not to overdo**
- **when to get help**

### Simple template
**Today:**  
[1 short sentence]

**Next steps:**  
- [Action 1]  
- [Action 2]  
- [Action 3]

**Watch for:**  
- [Sign 1]  
- [Sign 2]

**Follow-up:**  
[When/how to check in]

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-paragraph version** or a **fillable template** you can reuse for different clients.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty good. But I’d want it a little more practical — clients don’t always read a neat little block after they leave. Maybe make it even more usable with a quick top line like **‘Do these 3 things first’** and keep the wording plain, not clinical.

Also, for my kind of clients, it helps to include:
- **how often** to do the thing
- **how long** to keep it up
- **what means stop and call**

So maybe a format like:
**1. Main takeaway**
**2. 3 steps for the next few days**
**3. Warning signs**
**4. Next visit / when to check in**

If you want, make me a version that fits a massage/acupuncture client note, because that’d be the one I’d actually use.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — for a massage/acupuncture take-home note, I’d keep it **short, plain, and action-first**.

### Simple client note format

**Do these 3 things first**
- [Most important thing to do]
- [Second thing to do]
- [Third thing to do]

**Main takeaway**
- One sentence on what today was about and what they should expect

**Next 3 days**
- **Do:** [what], **how often:** [x/day], **how long:** [for ___ days]
- **Do:** [what], **how often:** [x/day], **how long:** [for ___ days]
- **Avoid:** [what to hold off on for now]

**Stop and call if**
- [warning sign 1]
- [warning sign 2]
- [warning sign 3]

**Next visit / check-in**
- [when to return]
- [when to message sooner]

### Example wording
**Do these 3 things first**
- Drink water and rest a bit today
- Do the gentle stretch we showed you, 2–3 times a day
- Keep activity light if the area feels sore

**Main takeaway**
- Today we worked on easing tension and helping your body settle after treatment.

**Next 3 days**
- **Do:** gentle stretching, 2–3 times/day, for 3 days
- **Do:** heat or ice if it helps, 10–15 minutes at a time
- **Avoid:** hard exercise or anything that makes symptoms flare

**Stop and call if**
- Pain gets worse instead of better
- You notice swelling, numbness, or unusual symptoms
- You feel unwell after treatment

**Next visit / check-in**
- Come back in [timeframe]
- Message sooner if things feel worse or don’t settle

If you want, I can make this into a **fillable one-page template** with blank lines you can reuse for different clients.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s closer. I’d still want it a little more “hand them something and they’ll actually look at it.” For my clients, the first line has to be blunt and useful — not just a heading.

Something like:

**Do these 3 things first:**
- [Action 1] — [how often]
- [Action 2] — [how often / how long]
- [Action 3] — [how long to keep it up]

Then keep the rest just as plain:

**Main takeaway:**
- One short sentence, no jargon.

**Next few days:**
- Do: ___, ___ times a day, for ___ days
- Do: ___, ___ minutes at a time
- Avoid: ___ until ___

**Stop and call if:**
- ___
- ___
- ___

**Next visit / check-in:**
- ___

For massage/acupuncture notes, I’d also maybe swap “warning signs” for “Stop and call if” — reads more direct. And I’d keep each bullet to one line if possible. A lot of people won’t read a paragraph once they’re out the door.

If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner one-page template with blanks that fits the way we actually write notes in practice.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that’s the right shape.

A **practical take-home note** for massage/acupuncture clients should be:

- **blunt up front**
- **plain language**
- **one line per bullet**
- include **how often**, **how long**, and **when to stop/call**

### Simple reusable format

**Do these 3 things first:**
- [Action 1] — [how often]
- [Action 2] — [how often / how long]
- [Action 3] — [how long to keep it up]

**Main takeaway:**
- [One short sentence, no jargon.]

**Next few days:**
- Do: [thing], [x] times a day, for [x] days
- Do: [thing], [how long each time]
- Avoid: [thing] until [timeframe]

**Stop and call if:**
- [warning sign 1]
- [warning sign 2]
- [warning sign 3]

**Next visit / check-in:**
- [when to return]
- [when to message sooner]

### Example
**Do these 3 things first:**
- Rest the area today — check in with it later
- Do the gentle stretch we showed you — 2–3 times a day
- Keep activity light for 2 days

**Main takeaway:**
- Today we worked on easing tension and helping things settle down.

**Next few days:**
- Do: gentle stretch, 2–3 times a day, for 3 days
- Do: heat or ice if it helps, 10–15 minutes at a time
- Avoid: hard exercise until tomorrow or until it feels settled

**Stop and call if:**
- Pain gets worse instead of better
- You notice swelling, numbness, or unusual symptoms
- You feel unwell after treatment

**Next visit / check-in:**
- Come back in [timeframe]
- Message sooner if symptoms worsen or don’t settle

If you want, I can make this into a **copy-paste clinic template** with blanks for fast use.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah — that’s basically it. The blunt first line and one-line bullets make it usable. I’d use that.

The example is fine too. Plain enough, no fluff.
